Tony Hoare passed away.

This quote has stuck with me since I first read it as a teen. It sparked a decade-long obsession with simplicity later expanded by Rich Hickey's Simple vs Easy.

"I conclude that there are two ways of constructing a software design: One way is to make it so simple that there are obviously no deficiencies, and the other way is to make it so complicated that there are no obvious deficiencies" https://en.wikiquote.org/wiki/C._A._R._Hoare#The_Emperor's_Old_Clothes

PPP-based pricing isn't a growth hack. For most of these new apps, it might be the difference between hitting $1K MRR and dying at $300.

Building this at PricePush. Making localized pricing a one-tap operation instead of a spreadsheet nightmare. Find out more: → pricepush . app

And here's the thing: most of these new apps launch with a single US-centric price and assume it'll work everywhere. They're leaving 80%+ of potential users priced out before they even try the product.

RevenueCat's new report confirms what I've been seeing: the apps that survive aren't the best-built ones. They're the ones that figured out pricing early. Speed to $1K MRR is the strongest predictor of long-term survival.

😂

A screenshot of text saying: Hello, wanting to provide context to why I need continuous help with survival funds.
I'm a single mom who also caretakes for my disabled child. He is high needs meaning he needs help with everything through out the day. I'm disabled myself. I have long covid which mostly entails POTs, ME/CFS and uterine fibroids. When I was last working I ended up in the ER from overworking my body and decided it was best for me to stay home especially since my disabled child was already struggling so much with the caretaker he had at the time. I also have a trans child so we live in California where it's safest for her and my family in general as two of us are disabled. I'm working towards getting on disability at the moment as well as exploring options to be paid to be my son's caregiver as his behavior gets worse when I'm not the one caring for him. Nothing goes unappreciated and I truly try to thank anyone who helps to the best of my ability. I suffer chronic fatigue so my spoons are limited. Thank you if you read this

external.description: 📌 Tails 7.6 has been released, introducing automatic Tor bridge retrieval for users on networks that block Tor access. The update enhances the Tor Connection assistant to detect restricted connections and automatically fetch region-specific bridges via the Tor Project’s Moat API, disguising requests as ordinary web traffic using domain fronting. Additionally, the release includes a new password manager feature. The changes aim to improve usability and privacy for users in censored environments. No specific CVE IDs or exact release dates beyond March 2026 were provided.

Anyone here use Microsoft FrontPage? ... last version was Frontpage
2003 ..... (self.abandonware)
submitted 1 year ago * by DudeSun_AG
I still use it to organize notes & data for my own large internal Wiki-type intranet site for WordPress web development & tech intel ... I could do the same thing with a local installation of WordPress, but a local WordPress version would require about 10x more work/hours to do the same thing ... and the FrontPage site doesn't require a bunch of plugins to do simple things like copying & pasting a basic page/file .... you could also build a big internal wiki with Microsoft Sharepoint - but Sharepoint is designed for larger/complex installations.
In my opinion ... the strength of FrontPage 2003 is; ... it's simplicity ... which allows for ultra quick/fast/easy use/execution.
I asked ChatGPT .....
* QUESTION ..... Is the discontinued software program by Microsoft named FrontPage 2003
considered abandonware?
* ANSWER ..... Yes, Microsoft FrontPage 2003 is generally considered abandonware. Abandonware refers to software that is no longer sold or supported by its developer, and FrontPage 2003 fits this description. Microsoft officially discontinued FrontPage in 2006, replacing it with Expression Web and later integrating web design features into Visual Studio.
Since there are no updates, support, or official channels for acquiring FrontPage 2003 anymore, it is considered abandonware. This means that while the software may still be available through unofficial channels, using it involves risks and limitations due to its lack of support and compatibility with modern systems

images.alt:
"I guess because I live in a place with almost zero praxis, it never occurs to me to think about such things," I said. "But at times like this, the absurdity hits me between the eyes. There's no reason to put up with junk like this. A stove with dangerous, unreliable chemical fuel. With orifices that clog. In four thousand years we could have made a better stove."
"Would I be able to take that stove apart and fix it?"
"You wouldn't have to, because it would never break."
"But I want to know if I could understand such a stove."
"You're the kind of person who could probably understand just
about anything if you set your mind to it."
"Nice flattery, Raz, but you keep dodging the question."
"All right, I take your point. You're really asking if the average
person could understand the workings of such a thing..."
"I don't know what an average person is. But look at Yul here.
He built his stove himself. Didn't you, Yul?"
Yul was uneasy that Cord had suddenly made this conversation about him. But he deferred to her. He glanced away and nodded.
"Yup. Got the burners from scavengers. Welded up the frame."
"And it worked," Cord said.
"I know," I said, and patted my belly.
"No, I mean the system worked!" Cord insisted.
"What system?"

She was exasperated. "The...the..."
"The non-system," Yul said. "The lack of a system."
"Yul knew that stoves like this were unreliable!" Cord said, nod-
ding at the broken one. "He'd learned that from experience."
"Oh, bitter experience, my girl!" Yul proclaimed.
"He ran into some scavengers who'd found better burner heads in a ruin up north. Haggled with them. Figured out a way to hook them up. Probably has been tinkering with them ever since."
"Took me two years to make it run right," Yul admitted.
"And none of that would have been possible with some kind of technology that only an avout can understand," Cord concluded.
"Okay, okay," I said, and let it drop there. Letting the argument play out would have been a waste of breath. We, the theors, who had retreated (or, depending on how you liked your history, been herded) into the maths at the Reconstitution, had the power to change the physical world through praxis. Up to a point, ordinary people liked the changes we made. But the more clever the praxis became, the less people understood it and the more dependent they became on us—and they didn't like that at all.

"I guess because I live in a place with almost zero praxis, it never occurs to me to think about such things," I said. "But at times like this, the absurdity hits me between the eyes. There's no reason to put up with junk like this. A stove with dangerous, unreliable chemical fuel. With orifices that clog. In four thousand years we could have made a better stove."
"Would I be able to take that stove apart and fix it?"
"You wouldn't have to, because it would never break."
"But I want to know if I could understand such a stove."
"You're the kind of person who could probably understand just
about anything if you set your mind to it."
"Nice flattery, Raz, but you keep dodging the question."
"All right, I take your point. You're really asking if the average
person could understand the workings of such a thing..."
"I don't know what an average person is. But look at Yul here.
He built his stove himself. Didn't you, Yul?"
Yul was uneasy that Cord had suddenly made this conversation about him. But he deferred to her. He glanced away and nodded.
"Yup. Got the burners from scavengers. Welded up the frame."
"And it worked," Cord said.
"I know," I said, and patted my belly.
"No, I mean the system worked!" Cord insisted.
"What system?"

She was exasperated. "The...the..."
"The non-system," Yul said. "The lack of a system."
"Yul knew that stoves like this were unreliable!" Cord said, nod-
ding at the broken one. "He'd learned that from experience."
"Oh, bitter experience, my girl!" Yul proclaimed.
"He ran into some scavengers who'd found better burner heads in a ruin up north. Haggled with them. Figured out a way to hook them up. Probably has been tinkering with them ever since."
"Took me two years to make it run right," Yul admitted.
"And none of that would have been possible with some kind of technology that only an avout can understand," Cord concluded.
"Okay, okay," I said, and let it drop there. Letting the argument play out would have been a waste of breath. We, the theors, who had retreated (or, depending on how you liked your history, been herded) into the maths at the Reconstitution, had the power to change the physical world through praxis. Up to a point, ordinary people liked the changes we made. But the more clever the praxis became, the less people understood it and the more dependent they became on us—and they didn't like that at all.

#crypto #cryptoetf #xrp

external.description: Wall Street is rapidly embracing tokenized assets, with XRP emerging as a potential bridge for this financial transformation. Major financial institutions are exploring blockchain-based versions of assets like stocks and ETFs, driving the need for efficient settlement infrastructure. Ripple and XRP are strategically positioned to facilitate this shift towards tokenized finance. A collaboration between Franklin Templeton and Ondo Finance indicates a move to tokenize real-world assets using the XRP Ledger. The partnership is already utilizing the XRP Ledger to introduce tokenized US Treasuries leveraging RLUSD for minting and redemption. Ripple has also partnered with Franklin Templeton and DBS Bank to explore tokenized fund trading and lending using sgBENJI and RLUSD. The XRP Ledger and RLUSD are key components enabling seamless asset movement within this ecosystem. Ripple is expanding its infrastructure in Africa, particularly around the XRP Ledger, including instant Naira payouts in Nigeria. Ripple is supercharging crypto-to-Naira payments through Redotpay, enabling users to receive local currency directly. Absa Bank in South Africa is now Ripple's first major custody partner on the continent. A pilot program with zero-knowledge privacy technology is underway on the XRPL testnet in Nigeria.
external.title: XRP Positioned At The Center Of Wall Street’s Tokenization Boom — Is A Rally Emerging?

Claude wird autonom 🤖 Das #KI System #Claude von #Anthropic kann jetzt eigenständig Computer steuern und Aufgaben wie Dateien senden oder Programme bedienen.

KI übernimmt Kontrolle 💻 Die Software nutzt Apps, Maus und Tastatur wie ein Mensch und führt Aufgaben automatisch aus, nachdem Nutzer zuvor ihre Zustimmung gegeben haben.

Wettlauf der Agenten 🚀 Mit dieser Funktion reagiert Anthropic auf den Boom autonomer #KIAgenten wie #OpenClaw, die eigenständig komplexe Aufgaben erledigen können.

Risiken bleiben bestehen ⚠️ Experten warnen, dass solche Systeme anfällig für Missbrauch, Datenzugriffe oder Angriffe sein könnten, wenn sie zu viel Kontrolle erhalten.

Erste Version mit Grenzen 🔄 Die Funktion befindet sich noch in der Testphase und sollte laut Entwickler nicht für sensible Daten genutzt werden, während Sicherheitssysteme weiter verbessert werden.

👉 eicker.TV ▹ / Tech / Media / Politik / Business ⏻ eicker.BEratung von Gerrit Eicker aus Münster

+ végső következtetést már nélkülük is elértem.

Így mutatta meg, hogy mi hiányzik belőle:

+ kiderült, hogy én, a kis műveletlen nő, ugyanazokon gondolkodom, mint a filozófusok, Kant vagy Whitehead, és sokszor ugyanarra az eredményre jutottam, mint ők - anélkül, hogy olvastam volna tőlük. Nem is tudnám őket olvasni előképzettség nélkül, de már nem vágok bele a tanulásba, minek is, ha a +

Vicces, hogy chatgpt mennyi érzelmet generál az emberekben. Nekem olyan, mint egy nagyon művelt barát, akivel elképesztő mélységben lehet bármiről beszélgetni, okos kérdéseket tesz fel, és gondolkodókat ajánl, akik hozzám hasonló következtetésekre jutottak. Valahogy kisebb lett tőle a világ, mert +
So, he’s functionally illiterate? Anybody shocked?
